free_text: Discovery of the Origin of the Enormous 88Zr
Neutron-Capture Cross Section and Quantifying Its
Impact on Applications
Main reference.
#doi:10.1103/PhysRevLett.134.112702
Experimental details.
Device for indirect capture
experiments on radionuclides (DICER) instrument at
the Los Alamos Neutrons Science Center (LANSCE).
A white source of neutrons for this system, spanning
the energy range from below thermal to above 1 MeV,
is produced when the pulsed 800 MeV proton beam from
the LANSCE linac impinges on the new Mark-IV
neutron-production target.
6Li-glass scintillators and
photomultiplier tubes. Backgrounds due to in-beam
gamma rays and scattered neutrons were measured and
subtracted. HPGe detector was used for the sample
characterization only.
The 88Zr was produced by irradiating an yttrium metal
target at the isotope production facility (IPF) at
LANSCE. The 88Zr was separated from the IPF
production target and fabricated into a sample in a
hot cell. To ensure a uniform amount of 88Zr across
the area of the neutron beam (1 mm diameter), the
88Zr was dissolved in hydrochloric acid (HCl) before
being loaded into the sample container. The amount of
88Zr in the sample (825+-33 MBq) was determined by
gamma-ray spectroscopy using a high-purity germanium
detector to count the decay gamma rays from its
daughter 88Y relative to 88Y standards that fabricated.
Container dimensions were measured with a precision
inspection device. In this way, the areal density of
the sample was determined to be (7.44+-0.22)*10**-7
atoms of 88Zr per barn. A sample-out or blank sample
was fabricated by dispensing HCl into a second
container.
Neutron energy is determined using the time-of-
flight technique. The transmission data for the 88Zr
sample can be converted to total cross section sigmat
= (ln(1/T88))/n, and numerically integrated above
Ecut=0.5 eV to obtain an estimate of the resonance
integral.
Approved by A.Stamatopoulos on March 27, 2025.
BP
1 standard-deviation statistical uncertainties.
Unfortunately, somewhat different amounts of HCl were
dispensed into the sample-in (7.8 mg) and sample-out
(8.1 mg) containers for 88Zr and there also was a
small difference (0.02 mm) between the inner
diameters of these containers. A correction to the
88Zr transmission, for these differences was
calculated using the measured weights and diameters,
and transmission data we obtained for the HCl sample.
This correction factor ranged from 0.85 at thermal
energy to 0.97 at 1 keV.
- Fig. 2
Data were received from A.Stamatopoulos on 3/25/2025.
The R-matrix formalism is a well-known technique for
fitting neutron transmission data and obtaining
resonance parameters. These parameters can then be
used to extract the neutron-capture cross section. At
these low energies, only two reactions are possible,
neutron scattering and neutron capture. Therefore,
each resonance has three parameters, its energy (E),
neutron width (Gn), and gamma radiation width (Ggamma).
Once these parameters are determined by fitting
transmission data, they can be used to calculate the
capture cross section.
Fitting uncertainties that include 1
standard-deviation statistical uncertainties.
Fixed value for elastic width in subthreshold
(negative energy) resonance.
- TableI
The neutron-capture cross section calculated with
SAMMY using the resonance parameters. Calculated from
the DICER results.
Errors include 1 standard-deviation
statistical uncertainties, fitting errors and the
largest uncertainty component is due to the Ggamma
for the subthreshold resonance.
-TableII
The neutron-capture cross section calculated with
SAMMY using the resonance parameters. Calculated from
the DICER results.
Errors include 1 standard-deviation
statistical uncertainties, fitting errors and the
largest uncertainty component is due to the Ggamma
for the subthreshold resonance.
-TableII
The cross section derived from the DICER neutron
transmission results. The cross sections are
reconstructed from the resonance parameters obtained
by SAMMY.
The other resonance-parameter uncertainties and an
additional 3% normalization uncertainty due to the
uncertainty in the sample areal density.
